import io
import string
import list

fn binary_search(_ nums: List<Int>, _ target: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut low = 0
  mut high = nums.len() - 1
  mut result: Option<Int> = None
  while low <= high
    let mid = low + (high - low) / 2
    let val = match nums.get(mid)
    when Some(v)
      v
    when None
      -1
    end
    if val == target
      result = Some(mid)
      low = high + 1
    else if val < target
      low = mid + 1
    else
      high = mid - 1
    end
  end
  result
end

fn main() -> Result<Unit, String>
  let line1 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end

  let line2 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end

  let target = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
  when Some(n)
    n
  when None
    0
  end

  let parts = string.split_whitespace(string.trim(line1))

  mut nums: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  while i < parts.len()
    let part = match parts.get(i)
    when Some(s)
      s
    when None
      ""
    end
    let n = match string.parse_int(part)
    when Some(v)
      v
    when None
      0
    end
    nums = list.push(nums, n)
    i = i + 1
  end

  match binary_search(nums, target)
  when Some(idx)
    print("index=#{idx}")
  when None
    print("not_found")
  end

  Ok(())
end
